title: Dan <> Jason @ GrowthX on B2B Measurement!

are you?

figure things always double in the last few days.

Until 1130?

guess I'll start with just for us.

And like we try to get people to text.

Try to attribute anything.

mean, we are getting to a point where the funnel is getting a little bigger.

a deal.

do.

know.
Yeah, I mean, basically all our customers are in that.

I think the challenge, yeah, I guess to have a tool that does it, but even just, like, getting access and buy-in to, like, roll something like that out, you know, because it touches so many sources of data.
Yeah.
That part seems challenging, I feel like.
Okay.
I think that gives me a good sense of where to show you.

mean, yeah, for the stuff we care about, like, touchpoint has to start with content, or at least be one of the touchpoints.

All right, so let me share with you kind of our approach, and then we can just dive into the product, and I'll show you some of the specific functionality, might make sense.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Okay, cool.
So, essentially, what we're trying to answer are these, like, age-old questions that still remain unanswered.
Marketing leaders want to know what's working, what's not working, how can I double down on things that work.
They bring this topic up a lot.
Where should I spend my next dollar?
Where does that go?
Where is the most efficient use of that money?
And then sometimes it's also where should I cut resources?
What's not working?
So we should just stop doing those.
So even though they have all these attribution models, they still can't answer those questions.
And the reason for that is the companies we're working with, have B2B, bigger B2B, that's a lot of Bs, bigger B2B deals that are complicated.
Lots of people involved as part of the buying group, and they take a long time to close.
And we commonly see thousand plus touch points on an account before a deal is closed.
Sometimes we had one the other day we looked at that 17,500 on one deal.
So lots of people doing a lot of things.
The way we've been trying to solve them, you're familiar with probably half or more of these.
First touch, last touch, W shape, J shape, medium.
Mixed modeling, multi-touch, come up with all kinds of different models to pick one or two touch points based on time.
So you're basing on the opportunity created date or the MQL date, you're trying to figure out, well, what do I give credit to if I just had to pick one based on rules?

The way we are thinking about this, we're calling it forensic attribution, essentially reading all of this stuff and understanding the context behind it, and then picking what are the handful of moments that really mattered.
So when we actually look at those deals, we can look at all the go-to-market efforts.

This is on the bottom of the graph, your webinars, your emails, your calls, and then on the top of the graph, you can see prospect engagement, and there's spikes of engagement where they're all of a sudden more excited.
They're taking meetings, they're responding to emails, and then usually an opportunity gets opened around that time, and we can detect what caused that to happen.
So the way we do that, first we take all the data, and this is what you were saying, Jason.
Thank you.
You get access to this data.
We just connect to it and extract it all and put it in a data warehouse.
Primarily, we get it from Salesforce and marketing automation.
Most of our customers are on HubSpot.
I guess half on HubSpot, half on Marketo.
And then we're able to find a lot of the other information through those platforms.
But we do have a direct gong integration, for example.
We pull transcripts from there.
Do you have Fathom?
What else?
Fathom, and then more on the website analytics side?
Yeah, so not Google Analytics.
We do have website analytics that comes from marketing automation.
So if they're running one of those, there's webpage visits, and then those have UTM parameters logged, usually in the logs, right?
If you actually go and dig deep into Marketo, you can find an activity log, the UTM parameters.
So I'll show you where that shows up.
On Fathom and other tools, as long as it...
If you to Salesforce anywhere in a custom object or an activity, we pull that.
So you're mostly, I think you said at beginning, but like very much like in B2B world, like even kind of like product into an upsell type of motion, like that wouldn't really work.
No, I don't think we work.
Right now we're not focused on the product.
We think we can do it, but I think it's a different problem entirely.
Yeah, it's mostly for the companies with bigger deals.
They're trying to get enterprise deals.
Okay, got it.
Once we get that data into one place, we heal it.
And this is where it really sets us apart from everybody else in the market.
We do things like buying group detection.

So if you only have one contact role on the opportunity, which is usually very common, we'll go find all the other people that should have been on the opportunity and treat them like a buying group based on their activity.
So we look at me.

records, call transcripts, and even if somebody says, hey, my boss, Jennifer, needs to approve of this before we proceed, we'll figure out who is that Jennifer and include that person as part of the buying group.
There's a lot of finding people, identity, discovery, and resolution here.
Sometimes the people don't even exist in your CRM, but they are part of the deal.
We figure out when things happened.
We merge and deduplicate activity across systems.
You might have like an email logged in HubSpot or Marketo, but you're also adding them to the Salesforce campaign that represents the same thing.
And then probably my favorite thing that we do is we extract missing touch points.
So this is reading actual emails and in transcripts and pulling out bits from it that are relevant to attribution.
So if somebody says, I searched this and I found your post, that can be tied to your organic channel, even if you don't have the webpage visit.
And then once we do all this stuff, we have a team of agents that crawls that can come up with qualitative answers on how did you get this deal?
We also then merge that into a quantitative view.
So you can aggregate this.
can look at it by like finding the touch points that mattered.
And this is just our first agent.
have other agents that we're creating based on customers' needs.
Like, I want to find the deals that had our AEs go on site before stage four.
And in order to do that, we have to look at account and address this and figure it out.

No, this is how the initial one we rolled out is how did it become a deal in the first place.
We call it our activation milestone.

So it takes a deal that exists and then starts researching around it and finding out who's involved and then what happened and kind of threads the needle all the way back to how did you actually get this person to get excited about a meeting.
Right.
Okay.

Um, and I'm sure myself thinks about deal that close because I don't actually spend that much time on that many deals unless they like need me to pitch something because I spend time on delivery.

Yeah.
So we actually have had deals where LinkedIn.
It was up because they said in the email, I saw your post on LinkedIn, or I read this post.
Or there's one, like it could come up on a call transcript.
So if say, hey, how did you hear about us?
And they say, yeah, I was on LinkedIn.
I saw this post that's being recorded.
It's in the call transcript.
That's actually really cool.
And it'll tie it back to the channel.
We had one that you would like.
Somebody on a call said, yeah, the reason why we were really interested was we saw a great LinkedIn ad.
And we kept seeing those ads over and over again.
I never clicked them, but seeing them.
And so that's why I thought I should request a demo.
And so their system had said, this was a demo request.
But our system said, no, it was LinkedIn ads on this one.
I think that's actually really cool.
Like taking the kind of implied touch points and like trying to extract that to, yeah, because like, I mean, you can't measure everything.
You can't, yeah, it's hard to do it.
And now we have AI that can help us do this.
So we're all in on that.
So to show you quickly a little bit different parts of the platform, we have our account explorer, which is the, we believe, the best way to visualize an account's history of all time.
It shows you the velocity line, which is how much activity is happening with your prospect.
So there's a little bit of interest, a little bit of interest, you can see not a lot, and then a lot of interest.
And these lines below are opportunities that are opened and then lost, or opened, moved through the funnel, and then won.
So red and green.
We have people who are engaged for the first time, and we can see what is their title and what do they do.
We can also engage with that to see their activity in the timeline.
And we can see, hey, there's somebody named Jen who got involved on November 30th, was super involved from that moment on, and then this activity just spiked up and an opportunity got opened.
That sounds really interesting.
What did Jen do?
How did we get Jen excited?
And then...
This is the touchpoint log, which is the chronological view of data from all your systems.
And we can see that Jen went to a chatbot on the website, filled out the chatbot, booked a meeting.
And then again, attribution would have probably said like, website visit, chatbot.
And it's hard to know how to invest more in chatbots.
But Jen also sent an email that says, I want to let you know, I've used your company before when I was at my last company.
I've been reading your blog.
And then here's a whole bunch of information about my company.
So there's a lot of hidden context in here that our agents will pick up.
And that you can see that here, where if we asked our agents, hey, how did we get this Hooli deal?
The answer is, this was a website chatbot engagement with this person, but because they have been reading the blog and they worked at another company.
And that comes with direct citations to your data.
So you can see, this is where we got that data.
We got all that context from these touchpoints.
And you can go validate.
So that's actually actually there.

So this is like the foundation of what we do, but if I transition over to, I guess the last thing I'll show you is like, on every deal, we'll see, like they only had one person on the opportunity, but we detected the other three that are also interesting that we should be tracking and looking at.
So yeah, so to go back to like, how do you report on a channel, right?
Because I think that's more your interest.
This is the foundation that shows it on an account.
We can look at this by a campaign.
So we can say like, I want to look at a specific conference that I did.
What results did I get from that conference?
What opportunities did I influence?
Which ones did the agents say I sourced?
So this is where we get some really creative stuff.

This was a $200,000 deal that eventually became closed one.
As of this conference, on the channel view, this is where we have, like, you can point this to, like, organic.
I don't know if this company has organic, but let's say, for example, content.
What this will do is collapse all the Salesforce campaigns into a specific channel and then run the analysis that we just looked at for all of them combined.
But you can also start filtering this of, like, I just want to know my tier one accounts.
How many of those did I affect?
How many of the specific industry or vertical did I affect?
So that, this is, like, channel view.
What we're building next, which will be live before our dinner, will be the website drill down.
So you can say, show me the results from this specific URL.
Or this group of URLs that we've, like, or we've created because of GrowthX.
Right.
And then...
I guess, like, how do you typically see customers using this?
Like, are they in here during their, like, pipeline review and stuff like that?
Or do they try to, like, take the data in here and, like, pipe it into HubSpot, you know, in some way?
Yeah, so far, yeah, they don't pipe it out.
Some have asked us to create integrations to write some stuff back to Salesforce.
And then when we ask them, what do you want written back?
They're not sure because there's a lot of data here.
Clear answers are, like, opportunity contact roles.
That would be nice to put back in Salesforce.
Maybe on a campaign report card, like, these tiles can exist on the campaign.
So you can just see this data there.
It's hard, though, because, like, you don't just want this number.
You then want to drill in and find out, well, what were these 13 opportunities that we influenced that became one?
And how can I drill in further and go look at the story and find exactly when this happened and validate that, like, this campaign really did bring that.
And campaign marketers use this view a lot.
So if they're running webinars, if they're running events, if they want to know, hey, did we do better this year or last year in this big conference?
There's like a benchmark mode where you can say, like, compare this event to all the other events that we did.
And then run, I'm going to just pick two random ones.
Basically create a benchmark and compare, like, should I do this or should I do that?
And this data can be exported.
You can merge it in with, like, cost data.
You can find out, you know, what you should really do.
So here's an example of, like, we just made a benchmark of some other data.
AEs and stuff, we'll use the Account Explorer to understand how accounts are progressing.
And if you want, like, a quick summary of what's going on in that account, you can just, like, quickly filter.
In your case, you might be interested in, like, I want to see the web activity for this customer.
might might interested in, be be in, in, in, in, you

And I can see, okay, somebody saw a webpage, workplace equity trends report, and then drill in further and look at UTM parameters.

Got it.
Yeah.
Here's an example.
Oh, this came from an email from Marketo.
Because I guess one of the hairy parts of trying to do this is stitching things together, like of who's who.
Yeah.
And what deals belong to, whatever.
But it seems like, like, does that happen here much?
Or is it happening because I have to get it into HubSpot first to get it into this platform?
So I have to figure it out there.
Yeah.
So here's an example.
This is part of our stitching.
We call it Genesis.
It's basically our whole foundation is built on a graph data model.
It's not a database.
So things are related based on context clues that we get.
So here's an example of that Gen D's person has a HubSpot contact record with the email address.
Yes.
Also a So.
Record also exists as a lead.
The lead has a weird name, but we detected it's the same person.
this case, they all have the same email.
This customer used to be on Marketo, and they moved on to HubSpot, and we've snapshotted their Marketo data, so they never lost that activity.
And now we have it.
So we also remember the history of the Marketo webpages.
And then we also see this person show up in Gong meetings.
And the last one is we've seen this person mentioned on email threads and referenced in other places.
So we stitch all of the profiles together to come up with a single person that we look at, a human.
In some cases, the emails don't match.
Let see if I can find one here.
They don't always match.
Sometimes it might be the personal and the corporate that are...
Yeah, I I think especially when you start doing, like, events.
Like, I mean, you look at the event we're holding, it's like a bunch of gmails in there.
Yeah, a bunch of gmails.
Exactly, yeah.
So we're...
This just keeps getting smarter and better.
Yeah.
But if you have your activity in different records, it's really hard to know what happened.
So that's basically what we've been doing for the past year, year and a half, is figuring out how do we develop the best view of what exactly happened.
That makes sense, but I feel like because I have to make sure, at least right now until something changes, that all this is also in the CRM, I kind of have to work through that problem already.

Like, for example, I need to have the event logged in HubSpot as an activity, and to do that, I have to struggle through this problem of exactly what your tool does, but only for your platform of being smart around, you know?
So I feel like, yeah, that part's kind of annoying, because I'll have to do it for HubSpot anyway, regardless.
But it's nice it's happening here automatically.
Oh, I see what you mean.
Yeah, the idea is, certainly, like, it's good to have good data hygiene and put things in the system, but nobody does perfectly.
Even when you look...
You're going to get the conference list like four days later and import it, you know, two days after that.
So now you're seven days behind.
And our system will kind of look at the context of the conference, understand when was it actually, and backdate those dates back to when it really happened and kind of use context clues.
I had one example where somebody was at the booth and they, at the booth, they requested a demo and they were able to detect that this wasn't a demo request.
Like the fact that there was an email that said, it was great seeing you at our booth.
And then it was also a demo request that probably happened at the event.
And this is an event conversion.
So, I mean, generally, the follow-up emails and like all those little clues that can help build the story, we can fill in a lot of the gaps.
Yeah.
That's part of the thing, but it's like, I guess most people are so ingrained to do this in HubSpot and they have like whatever segment and all this stuff, like trying to patch things up.
And then, I guess with your platform, what you're trying to do is, like, I should now do that for here in Upside.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That makes sense.
The activation agent is the first one that we have.
But, I mean, if you have questions, like, I want to know the deals that specifically brought up, like, this grouping of SEO pages or something.
Like, go through every deal and look for evidence of that and then prove to me which one of those are.
That's something that might take an analyst weeks to do, and our agents can do it in a few minutes.
Yeah.
That part's super interesting.
I mean, I I'm trying to think through, like, what touch points we do that you support.
Like, so on the GrowthX part, it's, like, a lot of stuff on social, which I think is hard.
I mean, I guess you'll see it through contextually in emails and stuff.
But understandably, can't, like, see until LinkedIn, you know?
Yeah.
Is there any way to pipe in custom activity?
We're working on this.
So if you pipe it in as a custom object, if you put it into Salesforce anywhere, you can easily extract that.
Okay, so I'll just pipe it into HubSpot and then it gets pulled into here.
Yeah, if you put it in HubSpot, yeah, that would work.
We're also working with some customers who are like, I just have this other bucket of data that maybe I could just give to you or send to you or something.
And we're working on that too.
Our vision is to take everything in.
This is just the beginning.
yeah, yeah.
That's kind of how I want to use it because HubSpot doesn't offer it, right?
For example, if I'm doing some weird thing where I'm scraping all of Marcel's posts, look at the engagement, right?
I guess if I have to put it into HubSpot first, I have to figure out who to attach it to.
And this problem of like, okay, now I have to figure out like Dan is this Dan and Dan, like random Dan.
So I already have to figure out the problem.
That your product solves, but because I can't, like, pipe it into your product, like, I have to go with this roundabout thing, you know?
Yeah.
We do get that question a lot.
They call it the shadow funnel.
Like, what's happening on LinkedIn, and how do I see that?
But, anyways, I know we're a few minutes over, and I want to let you go to your next thing.
This is really cool.
I mean, yeah, like, it's good to know what you guys do.

People like Unify.

that worked great.

like not too.

so between the four of us over a month, we got that many out.

So what I'll do, we're working on this like specific SEO tracking thing now or website tracking.

I think, yeah, I think on the client side, it's like designing it in a way where you don't have the sources.

I know this is not the use case you designed it for, but that's just how No, I've been thinking about it.
Basically, what you're describing is agency access.
Yeah.

But again, I know it's something you Are you guys SOC 2 compliant?

So from our first day, and sorry, I know we're way over, but I have time if you're okay.

don't know.

But not, I mean, again, I'll be part of it here.

It's like you were saying, you kind of know where your deals come from at that stage.
And then around 10 is when you start to lose track.
That's around 36K.
And then it goes up from there.
We're working with three companies that are in the business.
And it's more like in the millions.
So somewhere between that.
touch points kind of, I mean, you said what, like 20,000 touch points for one, I we're trying to process all of that.

Yeah, have, we have context window problems, for sure, because we want to give so much context, and we can't fit it all into one window, so then we have, that's why we went the multi-agent approach, where we have some agents that are really good at understanding what calls me, and others that are under the campaign, and then there's, like, a supervisor agent that tells them what to do, and signs up on their work, so it's, it ends up being a lot of calls, and it's not the, like, cheap LLM ones, it's the more advanced, expensive ones.

feel like where this goes is like, I mean, once you have all that information, you can make good decisions around it.

right.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss Upside's B2B measurement platform and explore potential applications for GrowthX and its clients.](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Upside offers a "forensic attribution" platform that analyzes touchpoints across the B2B sales cycle, providing insights into what drives deals](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1138.0)
  - [The platform uses AI to stitch together data from various sources (CRM, marketing automation, etc.) and extract insights from emails, call transcripts, and more](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [Pricing starts at $36K/year for companies with $10M+ revenue, scaling up based on data processing needs](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1495.0)
  - [GrowthX may explore using Upside for its own attribution needs and potentially recommend it to clients once they complete their HubSpot migration](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=2230.0)

## Topics

### Upside's B2B Measurement Platform

  - [Uses "forensic attribution" to analyze thousands of touchpoints across the B2B sales cycle](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1136.0)
  - [AI-powered agents extract insights from various data sources, including emails and call transcripts](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1138.0)
  - [Provides visualizations of account history, engagement, and key moments that influenced deals](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1144.0)
  - [Offers channel-level reporting and campaign effectiveness analysis](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1294.0)

### Data Integration and Security

  - [Integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, Gong, and other data sources](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [Uses graph data model to stitch together information from multiple systems](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1542.0)
  - [SOC 2 compliant with extensive security measures to protect client data](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=2340.0)

### Potential Applications for GrowthX

  - [Could help attribute results from content marketing and SEO efforts](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1283.0)
  - [Might be useful for showing ROI to clients, especially for hard-to-track channels like LinkedIn](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1779.0)
  - [Agency access model could allow GrowthX to pull reports for clients](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=2295.0)

### Pricing and Implementation

  - [Starts at $36K/year for companies with $10M+ revenue](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1495.0)
  - [Pricing scales based on data processing needs, which can be significant for large enterprises](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1492.0)
  - [Implementation requires access to various data sources, which can be a challenge with some clients](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=1866.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Dan to send Jason a demo video of Upside's upcoming website tracking feature](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=2243.0)
  - [GrowthX to consider trying Upside after completing their migration to HubSpot](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=2230.0)
  - [Jason to keep Upside in mind as a potential solution for GrowthX clients needing advanced B2B attribution](https://fathom.video/share/2XXxLmxwe5TfD1Tt36MfPAm3pFpNNxip?tab=summary&timestamp=2228.0)

action_items.description: Send demo video of website functionality to Jason when ready
Reach out to Dan about trying Upside platform after moving off Adio
